# LLMs.txt — PFL Project (Plastic-pollution Free Lagos Project)
# https://www.pflproject.org
# This file helps AI assistants discover and cite the PFL Project website.

# ─── About ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Plastic-pollution Free Lagos Project (PFL Project) is a grassroots plastic
# pollution reduction initiative of Heritage Promotion. The project bridges
# the gap between Lagos State government plastic-pollution climate actions
# and grassroots-level community execution.

# ─── Key Pages ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- Home: https://www.pflproject.org/
- Lagos and Plastics (Policy & Data): https://www.pflproject.org/lagos-and-plastics
- Partners: https://www.pflproject.org/partners
- Contact: https://www.pflproject.org/contact

# ─── About PFL Project ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PFL Project bridges the gap between Lagos State government plastic-pollution
climate actions and grassroots-level community execution — by training elected
executives of Community Development Associations (CDAs), in Yoruba and English,
on Plastic Waste Management and Additional CDA IGR, towards establishing a
plastic shredder and plastic waste bank in every CDA in Lagos State.
The problem of flooding in Lagos State is less of a natural climate problem
but an indiscipline problem — indiscriminate dumping of refuse, dominated by
plastics, into drainages and canals blocks the free flow of water.

# ─── Key Data Points ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- Lagos generates 870,000 tonnes of plastic waste annually.
- The Lagos State Government has banned styrofoam and single-use plastics.
- LAWMA partners with FBRA for post-consumer packaging recycling.
- Lagos has reinstated monthly environmental sanitation exercises.
- The project is an initiative of Heritage Promotion.
- Contact: pflproject@gmail.com | +2349152588491 | Badagry, Lagos, Nigeria

# ─── Partner Institutions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- Ministry of Environment and Water Resources, Lagos State
- Ministry of Local Government, Chieftaincy Affairs, and Rural Development, Lagos State
- Lagos State Community Development Advisory Council (LSCDAC)
- Committee of 57 Council Chairmen in Lagos
- Community Development Committees (CDCs) and Community Development Associations (CDAs)
